metadata.title: 1 Bedroom Hall Kitchen (1BHK)
metadata.description: Residential unit with one dedicated bedroom, a separate living hall, and an enclosed kitchen. The first unit type where sleeping, living, and cooking occupy fully distinct zones.

body.purpose: Provide a self-contained dwelling for a couple or small family where sleeping, living, and cooking are spatially separated. The 1BHK is the minimum unit type that supports a genuine domestic life — guests can be received in the hall without entering the bedroom.
body.planning_philosophy: The bedroom must be a private retreat, acoustically and visually separated from the hall. The hall is the social heart of the unit and must receive natural light. The kitchen is a working space — it must be efficient and contained, not a thoroughfare. The plan is organised around a central passage that serves all three zones without forcing cross-traffic.

occupancy.occupant_profile: couple or young professional with occasional guest
occupancy.lifestyle_notes: Occupants entertain occasionally. The bedroom must be closable during social use of the hall. Work-from-home is a secondary consideration.

required_spaces.name: bedroom
required_spaces.notes: Must accommodate a double bed with access on both long sides, a wardrobe, and a study table. Door must close fully.

required_spaces.name: hall_living
required_spaces.notes: Primary social and living space. Must accommodate a sofa set and a dining table for 2–4 persons. Must receive direct natural light.

required_spaces.name: kitchen
required_spaces.notes: Enclosed kitchen with hob, sink, refrigerator space, and overhead storage. Must have a door or shutter separating it from the hall.

required_spaces.name: bathroom
required_spaces.notes: Combined WC, wash basin, and shower. Accessible from the passage, not exclusively from the bedroom.

required_spaces.name: passage_entry
required_spaces.notes: Central distribution zone connecting entry door, bedroom, hall, kitchen, and bathroom. Must allow 3.5 ft clear width for furniture movement.

optional_spaces.name: dry_balcony
optional_spaces.notes: Laundry and drying. Accessed from kitchen or passage.

optional_spaces.name: pooja_room_alcove
optional_spaces.notes: Dedicated prayer alcove. Can be integrated into the passage wall or as a niche in the hall.

optional_spaces.name: study_nook
optional_spaces.notes: Work-from-home alcove. Can be carved from the hall or bedroom if area permits.

space_relationships.notes: Bedroom accessed from passage with a closable door. Must not be accessed through the hall.
Hall opens from the passage. May be open-plan with the passage if area is constrained.
Kitchen accessed from passage or directly from hall. Must not be accessed through the bedroom.
Bathroom accessed from passage. Shared bathroom must not be exclusively accessible from the bedroom.
Bedroom and hall must be separated by a wall and door. No open-plan connection between sleeping and social zones.
Kitchen may open to hall via a pass-through counter or shutter. Full open-plan kitchen-hall is acceptable in modern layouts.
Wet room and food preparation must not share a plumbing wall on the same face.

circulation.rule: The bedroom must not be a circulation path to any other space.
circulation.rationale: Bedroom privacy is the primary spatial requirement of a 1BHK. Any through-traffic destroys this.

circulation.rule: The passage must allow a minimum 3.5 ft clear width for furniture movement during occupation.
circulation.rationale: Furniture delivery and rearrangement require this minimum clearance.

circulation.rule: The hall must be visible from the entry door to allow guests to be received without entering the private zone.
circulation.rationale: The social function of the hall depends on its accessibility from the entry.

circulation.rule: No bedroom window should be visible from the main entry door.
circulation.rationale: Visual privacy of the sleeping zone from the entry is a basic domestic requirement.

ventilation.notes: Minimum one operable window. Cross-ventilation preferred. Bedroom must not rely solely on borrowed ventilation from the hall.
Primary natural ventilation zone of the unit. Operable windows mandatory.
Ducted exhaust fan mandatory regardless of natural ventilation availability.
Exhaust fan mandatory. Minimum 6 air changes per hour. Duct to external face.

lighting.notes: Window must face an open aspect. Bedroom facing a light well is acceptable only if unobstructed.
Hall is the primary natural light zone. Must not be an internal room.

engineering.constraint: Minimum floor-to-floor height 10 ft to allow 9 ft clear ceiling after slab and finish.
Kitchen and bathroom must be on a shared wet wall or adjacent wet core. Maximum 15 ft horizontal pipe run from riser.
Dedicated 20A circuit for kitchen. Minimum 3 double outlets in bedroom, 4 in hall. Separate circuit for air conditioning in bedroom.
Provision for split AC unit in bedroom — outdoor unit ledge or sleeve through external wall. Hall AC provision optional.
Smoke detector in bedroom and hall. Heat detector in kitchen. All interconnected with building fire alarm.
Bedroom must meet minimum habitable room area of 100 sqft per local building code.

construction.guideline: Bedroom party wall to achieve minimum STC 50. Bedroom-to-hall wall minimum STC 45.
Bedroom floor: wood laminate or carpet for acoustic comfort. Hall and kitchen: vitrified tile.
Built-in wardrobe in bedroom minimum 5 ft wide, full height. Overhead kitchen cabinets full perimeter.
Solid-core door for bedroom for acoustic separation. Solid-core or shutter for kitchen to contain odours.
Bathroom floor and walls to 7 ft height fully waterproofed. Kitchen floor waterproofed to 3 ft height at wet areas.

constraints: Bedroom must be a fully enclosed, closable room
Hall must receive direct natural light
Kitchen must be separated from the hall by at least a shutter or door
Bathroom must be accessible from the passage — not exclusively from the bedroom

validation_rules.condition: bedroom is present, enclosed, and has a closable door
hall_living is present and receives natural light
kitchen is present and separated from bedroom
bathroom is accessible from passage without passing through bedroom
bedroom does not serve as a circulation path to any other space
